findings.issue: Task 9 reads Payload Globals (navbar, footer, social-channels) via getPayload() Local API in new server components (NavbarServer.tsx, LeftSidebarServer.tsx, Footer.tsx). These Global definitions are created in Task 7 (wave 3). Task 9 is wave 5, so the dependency on Task 7 is correct and declared. HOWEVER: Task 9 also fetches Payload articles via `payload.find({ collection: 'articles' })` — the articles collection is defined in Task 6 (wave 3, depends_on: [3]). Task 9 declares depends_on: [3, 4, 5, 6, 7] which includes Task 6. This is correct. BUT Task 9 reads from lib/tags.ts and lib/posts.ts indirectly through app/articles/[slug]/page.tsx calling getArticleBySlug (MDX) and also directly calling payload.find — Task 9 does NOT depend on Task 8 (which rewrites lib/tags.ts and lib/posts.ts). Both Task 8 and Task 9 are wave 5. If a feature-execution agent runs both in parallel, Task 9 may read the old Prisma-based lib/posts.ts and lib/tags.ts while Task 8 is rewriting them, causing import-time conflicts or runtime errors. Task 9 should serialize after Task 8.
findings.fix: Add Task 8 to Task 9's depends_on: change from [3, 4, 5, 6, 7] to [4, 5, 6, 7, 8] and move Task 9 to wave 6. Task 9's article page also imports getPayload directly, which requires Task 3's Payload installation — already guaranteed transitively through Tasks 4-7. This finding was flagged as 'major' in cross-task-template-review.json but should be elevated to critical because app/articles/[slug]/page.tsx in Task 9 also calls lib/articles.ts indirectly, and lib/posts.ts (being rewritten by Task 8 simultaneously) is imported by multiple pages Task 9 modifies.

findings.issue: Task 10 creates app/api/articles/route.ts and declares depends_on: [8, 9]. The route uses `getPayload({ config })` with `import config from '@payload-config'`. The @payload-config alias is set up by withPayload() in next.config.mjs, which is done in Task 3. Task 10 does NOT declare depends_on Task 3. However, Task 8 depends_on [4, 5, 6, 7] and Task 4 depends_on [3], so Task 3 is transitively guaranteed. This is acceptable. The REAL cross-task issue: Task 10's Context Files section references `payload/collections/articles.ts` as 'создана в Task 7' — this is wrong. Task 6 creates articles.ts. Task 7 creates Globals (navbar, footer, socialChannels, siteSettings). This mislabeling means an agent executing Task 10 reading the context section will look at the wrong task for the articles collection definition, potentially using stale stub data instead of the full collection definition from Task 6.
findings.fix: In Task 10 Context Files section, change 'payload/collections/articles.ts — схема коллекции articles (создана в Task 7)' to '(создана в Task 6)'. Also update the comment 'Task 9 (App Routes + Globals Integration) — коллекция users с полем role (agent/admin/sync) должна существовать' — users collection is from Task 3, not Task 9. Fix dependency comment accuracy.

findings.issue: Task 11 rewrites scripts/sync-telegram.ts to use Payload REST API. The script calls `GET /api/tags?limit=200` (Tags REST endpoint) and `POST /api/posts` (Posts REST endpoint). Task 11 declares depends_on: [5, 8, 9]. However, Task 5 creates the Posts collection definition — the actual REST endpoint `/api/posts` is auto-generated by Payload based on the collection definition. For the endpoint to be available on staging for testing (Task 11 has no smoke test that actually calls the REST API — it only unit-tests mocked fetch), Task 11 implicitly requires Task 3 (Payload installation, which creates the REST API infrastructure). This is covered transitively through Task 5 → Task 3. The REAL critical issue: Task 11 depends_on Task 9, but Task 9 does NOT create or modify the Payload REST endpoints for posts or tags. Task 9 creates NavbarServer.tsx, LeftSidebarServer.tsx, and updates articles/page.tsx. The Task 11 dependency comment says 'Task 9 (App Routes + Globals Integration) — роуты и глобалы интегрированы, API Key режим включён'. API Key mode (useAPIKey: true on Users collection) is enabled in Task 3, not Task 9. The depends_on: [9] declaration for Task 11 appears to be justified by an incorrect rationale. Task 9 does not produce any artifact that Task 11 consumes.
findings.fix: Remove Task 9 from Task 11's depends_on. Task 11 needs: Task 3 (Payload + users collection with useAPIKey), Task 5 (Posts collection with sync role access), Task 8 (Prisma removal from sync script context). Update depends_on to [3, 5, 8] or keep [5, 8] since Task 3 is transitively guaranteed through Task 5. Remove the incorrect rationale comment. Fix the dependency comment to accurately state 'Task 3 — users collection with API Key enabled; Task 5 — posts collection with sync role access'.

findings.issue: Task 4 creates scripts/migrate-tags.ts which uses getPayload() Local API by importing from payload and payload.config.ts. The @payload-config alias is NOT available for scripts run via tsx outside the Next.js build context (withPayload() only configures this alias for the Next.js webpack build). Task 4's implementation hints correctly note: 'getPayload импортируется из payload, config — из ../../payload.config (относительный путь от scripts/)'. However, the task's TDD anchor mocks for migrate-tags.ts mock `getPayload` — but for the script to run in staging it needs the direct import path. This inconsistency is noted in batch3-r2-review.json as a minor hint issue, but elevating because Task 8's implementation hints say 'Импорт config: import config from @payload-config' while Task 4's hints correctly say to use relative path. Tasks 4 and 8 provide INCONSISTENT guidance for the same config import pattern — Task 4 (correctly) says relative path for scripts; Task 8 says @payload-config for lib/ files. An agent doing Task 8 may apply the relative path pattern from Task 4's context incorrectly to lib/ files.
findings.fix: Make the pattern explicit across tasks: (a) lib/* and app/* files: use `import config from '@payload-config'` (Next.js build context, alias works); (b) scripts/* files: use `import config from '../../payload.config'` (tsx context, no alias). Add this distinction to Task 8's implementation hints and ensure Task 4/5/11 script sections are consistent.

findings.issue: Task 12 (Code Audit) focuses on checking getPayload() calls in client components and afterChange hook correctness. Task 12 declares depends_on: [10, 11]. However, the audit checklist in Task 12, section 4 ('afterChange hook correctness'), checks the Posts collection hook: 'Posts collection: hook вызывает revalidatePath("/vacancies/[category]", "page") для основной категории поста'. This contradicts the actual hook strategy defined in tech-spec Decision 3 and implemented in Task 5: the hook should use `doc.slug` (the actual slug), not the Next.js template string '/vacancies/[category]'. The audit CHECKLIST itself contains the wrong expected value — if the auditor uses this checklist literally, they will mark a correctly-implemented hook (using doc.slug) as wrong, or worse, approve an incorrectly-implemented hook using the template string.
findings.fix: In Task 12 Focus Areas section 4 (afterChange hook correctness), change 'Posts collection: hook вызывает revalidatePath("/vacancies/[category]", "page") для основной категории поста' to 'Posts collection: hook вызывает revalidatePath(`/vacancies/${doc.slug}`, "page") using the actual document slug (not the Next.js route template string "/vacancies/[category]"), plus revalidatePath("/vacancies", "layout")'. This is the same correction needed in Task 5's actual implementation — both must use doc.slug per Decision 3.

findings.issue: Task 8 rewrites lib/tags.ts. The current lib/tags.ts exports getPostsByTag() — which is called from app/vacancies/[tagSlug]/page.tsx (via the getTagBySlug + getPostsByTag pattern). Task 8's 'All function signatures preserved' acceptance criterion lists getPostsByTag(tagSlug) in scope. However, Task 8's description also says getTagsWithCounts() should count posts using N+1 payload.find calls. The getTagsWithCounts function currently lives in lib/tags.ts, but the TDD anchor for Task 8 only has tests for getTagBySlug, not getTagsWithCounts. If the N+1 approach for getTagsWithCounts is implemented without a test, regressions in tag count display will go undetected. Task 14 (Test Audit) does call out 'getTagsWithCounts - returns array with count fields' as a required test, but Task 8's own TDD anchor doesn't include it — creating a gap between Task 8's delivery commitment and Task 14's expectation.
findings.fix: Add a TDD anchor test for getTagsWithCounts to Task 8: 'tests/unit/tags.test.ts::getTagsWithCounts - returns array with count fields — mock two payload.find calls (tags + posts per tag); verify result contains count property per tag'. This ensures the N+1 implementation is tested at Task 8 delivery time, not discovered as missing only when Task 14 runs.

findings.issue: Task 5's afterChange hook revalidation logic has an inconsistency with the overall revalidation strategy. Task 5's Details section shows the hook as: `if (doc.slug) { revalidatePath('/vacancies/${doc.slug}', 'page') }; revalidatePath('/vacancies', 'layout')`. But the category pages in this app are at /vacancies/[category] where [category] = tag slug, NOT post slug. Posts have their own slugs (used for post detail pages like /vacancies/[category]/[slug]). The afterChange hook for Posts revalidating `/vacancies/${doc.slug}` would revalidate a non-existent route pattern — post slugs are not category slugs. The correct revalidation for a post change is to invalidate the category page for each of the post's tags. This cross-task issue exists because Task 12's checklist (as flagged above) also perpetuates this confusion. Tech-spec Decision 3 says 'Post saved → revalidatePath("/vacancies/[category]", "page") for its primary category' — meaning the tag's slug, not the post's slug.
findings.fix: Update Task 5's afterChange hook strategy: instead of revalidating `/vacancies/${doc.slug}`, iterate over `doc.tags` (which is a relationship array) and call `revalidatePath('/vacancies/${tag.slug}', 'page')` for each populated tag, or at minimum for the primary tag (specialization type tag). If doc.tags is not fully populated in the hook context, use a shallow relationship and revalidate '/vacancies', 'layout' as a fallback. Add explicit note: 'doc.slug here is the POST slug, not a category slug — do not use it for /vacancies/ route revalidation'. Update Task 12's audit checklist accordingly.

findings.issue: Task 14 (Test Audit) depends_on: [8, 10, 11] but NOT Task 9. Task 14's Expected Coverage includes tests for lib/postUtils.ts::getPrimaryCategorySlug and FeedPost alignment. Task 9 adds mergeAndSortArticles to lib/articles.ts (or a new file) and tests for it in tests/unit/articles.test.ts. Task 14's Expected Coverage does not include auditing articles.test.ts or the mergeAndSortArticles function — so when Task 9's code and tests are delivered, Task 14 will not verify their adequacy. The TDD anchor in Task 9 specifies 4 tests for mergeAndSortArticles, but Task 14 won't check if they actually cover the merge logic correctly.
findings.fix: Add articles merge function to Task 14's Expected Coverage: 'lib/articles.ts (mergeAndSortArticles): sorting by publishedAt desc, missing date at end, correct shape, empty Payload array'. Add depends_on Task 9 to Task 14, or add a note that articles merge tests are checked in Task 9's reviewer scope rather than Task 14.

findings.issue: Task 6 creates payload/collections/media.ts with upload config `staticDir: 'public/uploads'`. Task 9's NavbarServer and LeftSidebarServer do not consume Media collection. However, Task 7's siteSettings.ts global has a field `defaultOGImage: upload, relationTo: 'media'`. Task 7 depends_on [3] but NOT Task 6. If Task 6 and Task 7 run in parallel (both wave 3), siteSettings.ts is created while media.ts is still a stub. At runtime, Payload validates relationships at startup — if media collection stub has no upload config, the SiteSettings global may fail to initialize properly. Task 7's edge cases note 'TypeScript may not find the type, but npm run build won't fall' — this is accurate for build time, but Payload runtime registration could fail if the media stub is incomplete.
findings.fix: Add a note in Task 7 that siteSettings.ts's `defaultOGImage` upload field will be fully functional only after Task 6 completes. The wave ordering guarantees they both complete before wave 4, so no actual execution hazard exists — but document the dependency for clarity. Consider adding Task 6 to Task 7's depends_on if strict ordering of wave-3 tasks is required.

findings.issue: Task 16 describes deploy.yml steps in Step 4 as if they are already implemented (mentioning 'npx prisma generate' as 'старый шаг'). This description implies the agent should be reading the deploy.yml that has ALREADY been updated by Task 2. However, Step 4 is labelled as a passive 'what will happen' description — an agent might confuse it with the actual current state of deploy.yml. If Task 2 was not completed, the agent proceeding through Step 4 without checking the file would trigger a deploy without payload migrate running, leading to a broken deployment. The cross-task-template-review.json already flagged that Task 16 doesn't have Task 2 in depends_on. This is a compound risk.
findings.fix: Add Task 2 to Task 16's depends_on frontmatter (currently only [2, 15] — wait, checking: Task 16 frontmatter says depends_on: [2, 15] — this IS correct. The frontmatter already lists Task 2. The issue is only in the Step 4 description clarity. No frontmatter fix needed. Add an explicit verification step: 'grep -n "payload migrate" .github/workflows/deploy.yml — must return results; if not, Task 2 is incomplete, abort deploy.'

getPayload_singleton.status: OK — all consumers depend on Task 3 directly or transitively. No duplicate initialization. getPayload() is a Payload-managed singleton per process.
getPayload_singleton.note: Tasks 4 and 5 migration SCRIPTS initialize getPayload() at runtime (tsx context), not build time. They must run sequentially to avoid race conditions on the shared PostgreSQL pool. Task 5 (wave 4) now correctly depends_on Task 4 (per previous template review fixes).

postgresql_pool.status: OK — single pool per process. Migration scripts (Tasks 4 and 5) are NOT run in parallel by feature-execution agents; they are CLI scripts run manually at deploy time. Wave ordering doesn't apply to migration script execution.
postgresql_pool.residual_risk: Task 4 and Task 5 collection DEFINITIONS fill stubs in parallel — this is fine (no DB access during code writing). The migration scripts themselves must be run sequentially, which is documented in Task 16 Step 5.

payload_rest_api.owner: Payload auto-generated endpoints (active after Task 3 + Task 16 deploy)
payload_rest_api.status: OK — REST API is consumed only in staging/production contexts (Tasks 11, 15, 16, 17), not in code-writing waves.

afterChange_hooks.issue: Tasks 4, 5, 6, and 7 each add afterChange hooks calling revalidatePath(). All use the same pattern (import from next/cache). No shared state between hooks — each fires independently per save event. No cross-task resource conflict.
afterChange_hooks.critical_pattern_bug: Task 5 hook uses doc.slug (post slug) for /vacancies/ revalidation — this is WRONG; should use tag slugs from doc.tags. See major finding on Task 5.

wave_graph_verified.note: Wave graph reflects actual task frontmatter values. Task 5 is wave 4 (corrected from original wave 3 per previous template review). Task 8 and 9 are both wave 5 — see critical finding on Task 9 about needing Task 8 as a dependency.

inconsistent_patterns_same_wave.issue: Task 8 and Task 9 both call getPayload() in server context. Task 8 uses `import config from '@payload-config'` (correct for lib/ files). Task 9's NavbarServer and Footer use `getPayload({ config })` with the same alias. Pattern is consistent. No inconsistency.

All three tasks fill Payload collection/global stubs created in Task 3. All use the same CollectionConfig/GlobalConfig pattern with afterChange hooks importing from 'next/cache'. Pattern is consistent across the wave.

Task 12 is code-reviewing skill, Task 13 is security-auditor skill, Task 14 is test-master skill. All three are audit tasks that read code and write to decisions.md. They use different skills for different concerns — this is intentional specialization, not an inconsistency.

import_dependency_verification.dependency_declared: Task 10 depends_on [8, 9]; Task 8 depends_on [4,5,6,7] which includes Task 6; Task 3 transitive through Task 6
import_dependency_verification.status: OK transitively — but Task 10 Context Files mislabels articles.ts as 'created in Task 7' (it's Task 6). Hallucination in documentation.

import_dependency_verification.imports: scripts/sync-telegram.ts reads lib/tag-matcher.ts (unchanged), no Payload config import (uses REST API, not Local API)
import_dependency_verification.dependency_declared: N/A — lib/tag-matcher.ts is not created by another task in this feature
import_dependency_verification.status: OK — lib/tag-matcher.ts already exists in codebase
